title: How Munched Is That Birdie In The Window

jokes.text: Oh, don't be scared, boys. That's just the angels bowling.
jokes.setup: Children are scared by thunder sounds
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent, childlike explanation for thunder
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge - sweet, innocent way of explaining scary things to children. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: And so The Bloody Hangman, his hair sticky with blood, skittered along the cobblestones, gunk dripping from his one good eye, singing his evil land chantey.
jokes.setup: Homer is telling what should be a bedtime story
jokes.punchline: Extremely gruesome, inappropriate content for children
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - completely oblivious to what's appropriate for kids. The contrast between bedtime story format and horror content works well.

jokes.text: ♪ Ker-shaw, ker-shew ♪ ♪ I'm after you ♪ ♪ With my bloody noose ♪ ♪ Strong enough to hang ten moose ♪
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'noose' with 'ten moose' - absurd specificity
jokes.explanation: The specific measurement of 'ten moose' is wonderfully random and Homer-esque.

jokes.text: Homer, are you scaring those kids? No, I'm not! Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if Homer is scaring kids
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately contradicts his denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that completely capture Homer's inability to lie convincingly.

jokes.text: You said it was Boston. Did I?
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual indifference to his own story inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's complete lack of commitment to narrative consistency - very true to character.

jokes.text: Bodies piled up like those stackable plastic chairs.
jokes.punchline: Mundane comparison to school furniture
jokes.explanation: The juxtaposition of horror with banal school furniture is perfectly Homer-esque observational humor.

jokes.text: Like those chairs in the music room? Possibly!
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual 'Possibly!' to child's question
jokes.explanation: Homer's enthusiastic uncertainty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: The Hangman's here! ♪ Ker-shaw, ker-shew, my lies have come true! ♪
jokes.setup: Kids think the story character is real
jokes.punchline: Homer accidentally admits he was making it all up
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Homer accidentally breaks the fourth wall of his own story. 'My lies have come true' is wonderfully self-aware.

jokes.text: Huh. It's a pigeon.
jokes.setup: Built up terror about the Bloody Hangman
jokes.punchline: Mundane reality - just a pigeon
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - all that buildup for a harmless bird. Homer's casual delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I bet it's a combination to a safe!
jokes.setup: Finding a number on the pigeon
jokes.punchline: Bart's wild leap to exciting conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's imagination and tendency toward schemes.

jokes.text: Ask about the safe. No, better yet, act like you know about the safe.
jokes.setup: Bart wants to pursue his safe theory
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalates to pretending he already knows
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's scheming nature and tendency to overcomplicate.

jokes.text: He's a homing pigeon, girlie. Why don't you let him fly home? Unless you want to spring for a limo.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic limo suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm from a cranky character, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: Wherever I go, 200 birds gotta come with me. And they all want to sit in the front seat with Daddy.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't come get the bird
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of demanding pigeons wanting front seat
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist image of entitled pigeons. The 'Daddy' detail sells it.

jokes.text: Me? Just what in my long, sad history with frogs makes you think I can take care of a bird?
jokes.setup: Marge suggests Bart care for the bird
jokes.punchline: Reference to Bart's past pet disasters
jokes.explanation: Good continuity joke that acknowledges Bart's track record with pets.

jokes.text: Listen, don't tell anyone, but... I can't stand pigeons! They're like rats with wings!
jokes.punchline: Reveals unexpected prejudice with classic comparison
jokes.explanation: 'Rats with wings' is a common expression, so points off for originality, but works for character contrast.

jokes.text: He's crawling on your arm.
jokes.punchline: Pigeon is already on her, making her fears immediate
jokes.explanation: Good timing - her fear is immediately tested. Visual comedy works well.

jokes.text: Oh! No! No! No! Get off! Get off! Get off!
jokes.setup: Lisa's brief acceptance of the pigeon
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to panic
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation but works for the sequence.

jokes.text: You and I have shared too many seed bells to just walk away from this.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to release his pet pigeon
jokes.punchline: Treating bird feeding like shared romantic memories
jokes.explanation: Great line that treats pet care like a romantic relationship. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Go ahead, make fun of me. I lost my heart to a bird.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of unusual bond
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with self-deprecating humor.

jokes.text: Haw-haw. Man, you were not into that. What's wrong?
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on his own lack of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Nelson critiques his own performance of his signature trait. Self-aware and character-perfect.

jokes.text: My mom ran off with my birthday clown.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's not enthusiastic about bullying
jokes.punchline: Absurdly tragic backstory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson backstory - absurd enough to be funny but tragic enough to explain his character. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: That coop looks a little flimsy. Flimsy? This gentle pat says different.
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'gentle pat' presumably destroys it
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overconfidence followed by immediate failure. Standard but effective.

jokes.text: But don't feel bad. You're ahead of Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Explaining pecking order to the bird
jokes.punchline: Milhouse ranks below a bird
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Milhouse abuse running gag. Shows Bart's casual cruelty to his best friend.

jokes.text: I thought your race car was a Transformer! Nothing's one thing anymore!
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis about toy complexity
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy about modern toy marketing. 'Nothing's one thing anymore!' is very quotable.

jokes.text: Now? Do you forgive me now?
jokes.setup: Milhouse waiting behind fence for forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Persistent, immediate follow-up
jokes.explanation: Classic needy Milhouse behavior. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Bored by black and white footage of World War II? Recently uncovered color footage will get you back in the game!
jokes.punchline: Treating war footage like entertainment to be spiced up
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how media packages historical tragedy as entertainment.

jokes.text: Order this DVD now, featuring dictator commentary by Hitler and Mussolini.
jokes.punchline: Absurd idea of dead dictators providing commentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating war criminals like DVD commentary hosts.

jokes.text: There's a bomb in my helmet.
jokes.setup: Pigeon delivering message to General Patton
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely casual delivery of urgent information
jokes.explanation: Absurd message content delivered matter-of-factly. Good economy.

jokes.text: Caesar can have his eagle, Alexander the Great his gryphon... I'll take the American fighting pigeon any damn day!
jokes.punchline: Elevating pigeons above mythical war animals
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation putting humble pigeons alongside legendary war symbols.

jokes.text: Is he good at tic-tac-toe, like that chicken I played, lost to, and ate at the State Fair?
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to eating a chicken that beat him at games
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer - loses to a chicken, eats it anyway. Dark but hilarious escalation.

jokes.text: Mom, why am I so repulsed by pigeons? It doesn't make any sense. I'm a member of People for the Ethical Treatment of Amoebas, The League of Women Vultures, the NAAC Porcupines, Kids For Squids, the ACL Unicorns, Clamnesty International
jokes.punchline: List of ridiculous animal rights parody organizations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant string of organization name parodies. 'Clamnesty International' is particularly clever.

jokes.text: Indiana Jones had snakes, the Grizzly Man had grizzlies, and... you know, I'm not crazy about opossums.
jokes.setup: Explaining that everyone has animal fears
jokes.punchline: Marge's comparatively mundane opossum fear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - from movie hero fears to death to Marge's mild discomfort.

jokes.text: Opossum!
jokes.punchline: Immediate fearful reaction to Maggie
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback - her fear is tested instantly. Great economy.

jokes.text: Every day since we broke up is the greatest day of my life.
jokes.punchline: Brutal insult disguised as romantic message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - sounds romantic until you process the meaning. Very quotable insult.

jokes.text: She was on the rebound from Willie.
jokes.punchline: Making it worse by mentioning she rebounded from Willie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - makes Skinner feel even worse by adding more context.

jokes.text: Perhaps an update on the siege of Khartoum. 'Do a ballet dance without no clothes on.'
jokes.punchline: Gets juvenile prank message with bad grammar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - his old-timey expectations clash with modern prank reality.

jokes.text: Bad grammar, good advice.
jokes.setup: Reading the poorly written prank message
jokes.punchline: Burns' refined assessment of crude suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - aristocratic assessment of vulgar suggestion. Great economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Hey, if it moves, you can bet on it. What about the Detroit Lions? Now, now, lay off Detroit. Them people is living in Mad Max times.
jokes.setup: Discussing what can be bet on
jokes.punchline: Detroit being so bad it's post-apocalyptic
jokes.explanation: Good topical humor about Detroit's struggles. 'Mad Max times' is very quotable.

jokes.text: Danica Patrick in my thoughts! That's right, Homer. I'm contractually obligated by my sponsors to appear in random fans' fantasies.
jokes.punchline: Celebrity acknowledging contractual fantasy appearances
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about celebrity endorsements extending to fans' mental fantasies.

jokes.text: You Brickyard bimbo!
jokes.setup: Marge catching Homer's Danica Patrick fantasy
jokes.punchline: Racing-specific insult
jokes.explanation: Good alliteration and racing-specific jealousy from Marge.

jokes.text: What they don't suspect is that I'm into this.
jokes.setup: Being caught in fantasy/punished by Marge
jokes.punchline: Revealing he enjoys the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - finding unexpected pleasure in punishment.

jokes.text: There's no such thing as a Birditarod. It was just a trick to scam you out of your house.
jokes.punchline: Moe admits the whole thing was a scam
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - casual admission of attempted fraud. Good misdirection payoff.

jokes.text: Well, ask your new father.
jokes.punchline: Implying Homer isn't really Bart's father
jokes.explanation: Standard marital sarcasm from Marge.

jokes.text: This funeral just got depressing.
jokes.punchline: Implying funerals aren't normally depressing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan line. Perfect Homer logic - funerals becoming depressing only now.

jokes.text: It's from Bye Bye Birdie.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the coincidental appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-reference to the musical's title fitting the situation.

jokes.text: Not only have his grades gone up, but the enthusiasm has gone out of his pranks.
jokes.punchline: Academic improvement vs. lost prank enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good insight into what makes Bart special from an educator's perspective.

jokes.text: Marge, listen to the man; he pays Bart's salary. No, he doesn't. Why can't you support my gibberish?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to support the principal
jokes.punchline: Admitting his statement was nonsense and asking for support anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - self-aware about his nonsense but demanding support anyway. 'Support my gibberish' is incredibly quotable.

jokes.text: I'd do it if you were stupid.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for support of his gibberish
jokes.punchline: Marge's backhanded conditional support
jokes.explanation: Great Marge comeback - perfect economy and logic.

jokes.text: Hey, in this economy, I'll even remove tattoos. Even my tramp stamp?
jokes.punchline: Homer's personal interest in tramp stamp removal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer having a tramp stamp is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I bought it because Catherine Zeta-Jones told me to.
jokes.punchline: Marge's celebrity endorsement vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on celebrity endorsement effectiveness.

jokes.text: See? He's a questionable character. He used to hang around racetracks, you know.
jokes.punchline: Dog appears to respond with damaging information
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the dog's backstory and visual gag of him seemingly talking back.

jokes.text: As sure as I am that I'll be billing you for that torn magazine. Oh, that's pretty sure. Subscription rate, right? Newsstand.
jokes.punchline: Homer's realization about expensive newsstand rate
jokes.explanation: Good escalating financial worry from Homer.

jokes.text: And for once, I'm not talking about Lisa.
jokes.setup: Asking why something is coming with them
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he means the dog, not Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry joke with nice self-awareness about the show's running dynamic.

jokes.text: It's amazing how I can feel sorry for you and hate you at the same time. I'm sure there's a German word for it.
jokes.punchline: German language complexity stereotype
jokes.explanation: Smart Lisa line that accurately captures sibling relationships and German language humor.

jokes.text: Hmm. Backyard, backyard, toilet, ocean, don't know, backyard, Flanders' mailbox, Lenny's freezer, tire fire.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing disposal methods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation. Each disposal method gets progressively worse and more specific. Very quotable list.

jokes.text: Well, 'ditching' is a harsh word. Enough chitchat. Time for Operation Dog Dump.
jokes.punchline: Immediately using 'Dog Dump' which is worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - objecting to mild term while using harsher one.

jokes.text: We've shared a lot of great memories, like the time we got our picture in the paper with the caption 'Who's walking who?' I mailed them the answer, but they never printed it.
jokes.setup: Bart remembering good times with the dog
jokes.punchline: Taking newspaper caption literally and responding
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Bart's literal interpretation of rhetorical questions.

jokes.text: All right, you, give me back my phone and nobody gets hurt! You said nobody gets hurt! Those were your exact words!
jokes.punchline: Ostrich apparently responding to logical argument
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer treating ostrich like a reasoning being.

jokes.text: Help! Kill the bird! Never kill a bird! Never kill a bird!
jokes.punchline: Bart's moral conflict between helping dad and bird ethics
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bart's principles conflict with emergency situation.

jokes.text: Well, boy, I guess I owe you an apology. Who's a bird-eating monster? You are! You are! Yes, you are.
jokes.punchline: Apology delivered in baby talk format while calling him a monster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of baby talk delivery with 'monster' content. Perfect Bart moment.

jokes.text: Well, Bart learned a lesson, and most important of all, we don't have to stop and pick up dinner.
jokes.setup: Wrapping up the episode's moral lesson
jokes.punchline: Practical benefit of having fresh ostrich meat
jokes.explanation: Good dark ending joke - turning violent encounter into dinner convenience.

tmdb_overview: After Bart nurses an injured bird back to health, Homer enters it in a race.
